    55→docs = [

    57→    "sphinx-rtd-theme>=0.5.0",
    58→    "nbsphinx>=0.8.0",
    59→    "jupyter>=1.0.0",
    60→]
    61→
    62→[project.urls]
    63→Homepage = "https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/pyfmrihrf"
    64→Documentation = "https://pyfmrihrf.readthedocs.io"
    65→Repository = "https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/pyfmrihrf"
    66→Issues = "https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/pyfmrihrf/issues"
    67→
    68→[tool.hatch.build.targets.sdist]
    69→include = [
    70→    "/pyfmrihrf",
    71→    "/tests",
    72→]
    73→
    74→[tool.pytest.ini_options]
    75→testpaths = ["tests"]
    76→python_files = ["test_*.py"]
    77→python_classes = ["Test*"]
    78→python_functions = ["test_*"]
    79→addopts = [
    80→    "--strict-markers",
    81→    "--tb=short",
    82→    "--cov=pyfmrihrf",
    83→    "--cov-report=term-missing",
    84→    "--cov-report=html",
    85→]
    86→markers = [
    87→    "benchmark: marks tests as performance benchmarks (deselect with '-m \"not benchmark\"')",
    88→    "slow: marks tests as slow (deselect with '-m \"not slow\"')",
    89→    "cross_test: marks tests that require R fmrihrf package",

    91→

    94→warn_return_any = true
    95→warn_unused_configs = true
    96→disallow_untyped_defs = true

    99→disallow_untyped_decorators = true

file.content:
[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[project]
name = "pyfmrihrf"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Python implementation of fMRI Hemodynamic Response Function (HRF) and Regressor Tools"
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.8"
license = {text = "MIT"}
authors = [
    {name = "Bradley Buchsbaum", email = "brad.buchsbaum@gmail.com"},
]
keywords = ["fMRI", "HRF", "neuroimaging", "hemodynamic response", "neuroscience"]
classifiers = [
    "Development Status :: 3 - Alpha",
    "Intended Audience :: Science/Research",
    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering :: Medical Science Apps.",
    "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.8",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.9",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
]

dependencies = [
    "numpy>=1.21.0",
    "scipy>=1.7.0",
    "pandas>=1.3.0",
    "scikit-learn>=0.24.0",
    "numba>=0.54.0",
    "matplotlib>=3.4.0",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest>=6.2.0",
    "pytest-cov>=2.12.0",
    "hypothesis>=6.14.0",
    "black>=21.6b0",
    "mypy>=0.910",
    "rpy2>=3.4.0",
    "ruff>=0.1.0",
    "pre-commit>=2.15.0",
]
cross-test = [
    "rpy2>=3.4.0",
    "pytest>=6.2.0",
    "pytest-json-report>=1.5.0",
    "pytest-benchmark>=3.4.0",
]
docs = [
    "sphinx>=4.0.0",
    "sphinx-rtd-theme>=0.5.0",
    "nbsphinx>=0.8.0",
    "jupyter>=1.0.0",
]

[project.urls]
Homepage = "https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/pyfmrihrf"
Documentation = "https://pyfmrihrf.readthedocs.io"
Repository = "https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/pyfmrihrf"
Issues = "https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/pyfmrihrf/issues"

[tool.hatch.build.targets.sdist]
include = [
    "/pyfmrihrf",
    "/tests",
]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
python_files = ["test_*.py"]
python_classes = ["Test*"]
python_functions = ["test_*"]
addopts = [
    "--strict-markers",
    "--tb=short",
    "--cov=pyfmrihrf",
    "--cov-report=term-missing",
    "--cov-report=html",
]
markers = [
    "benchmark: marks tests as performance benchmarks (deselect with '-m \"not benchmark\"')",
    "slow: marks tests as slow (deselect with '-m \"not slow\"')",
    "cross_test: marks tests that require R fmrihrf package",
]

[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.8"
warn_return_any = true
warn_unused_configs = true
disallow_untyped_defs = true
disallow_incomplete_defs = true
check_untyped_defs = true
disallow_untyped_decorators = true
no_implicit_optional = true
warn_redundant_casts = true
warn_unused_ignores = true
warn_no_return = true
warn_unreachable = true
strict_equality = true

[tool.black]
line-length = 88
target-version = ['py38']
include = '\.pyi?$'

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 88
select = [
    "E",  # pycodestyle errors
    "W",  # pycodestyle warnings
    "F",  # pyflakes
    "I",  # isort
    "B",  # flake8-bugbear
    "C4", # flake8-comprehensions
    "UP", # pyupgrade
]
ignore = []
target-version = "py38"

[tool.ruff.isort]
known-third-party = ["numpy", "scipy", "pandas", "sklearn", "numba", "matplotlib"]

× This environment is externally managed
╰─> To install Python packages system-wide, try brew install
    xyz, where xyz is the package you are trying to
    install.
    
    If you wish to install a Python library that isn't in Homebrew,
    use a virtual environment:
    
    python3 -m venv path/to/venv
    source path/to/venv/bin/activate
    python3 -m pip install xyz
    
    If you wish to install a Python application that isn't in Homebrew,
    it may be easiest to use 'pipx install xyz', which will manage a
    virtual environment for you. You can install pipx with
    
    brew install pipx
    
    You may restore the old behavior of pip by passing
    the '--break-system-packages' flag to pip, or by adding
    'break-system-packages = true' to your pip.conf file. The latter
    will permanently disable this error.
    
    If you disable this error, we STRONGLY recommend that you additionally
    pass the '--user' flag to pip, or set 'user = true' in your pip.conf
    file. Failure to do this can result in a broken Homebrew installation.
    
    Read more about this behavior here: <https://peps.python.org/pep-0668/>

note: If you believe this is a mistake, please contact your Python installation or OS distribution provider. You can override this, at the risk of breaking your Python installation or OS, by passing --break-system-packages.
hint: See PEP 668 for the detailed specification.

1→"""Derivative methods for HRF objects."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→from typing import Union
     6→import numpy as np
     7→from numpy.typing import ArrayLike, NDArray
     8→from scipy.misc import derivative
     9→
    10→from .core import HRF
    11→
    12→
    13→def deriv(hrf: HRF, t: ArrayLike, method: str = "auto") -> NDArray[np.float64]:
    14→    """Compute the derivative of an HRF at given time points.
    15→    
    16→    Args:
    17→        hrf: HRF object to differentiate
    18→        t: Time points at which to evaluate the derivative
    19→        method: Derivative method ('auto', 'numeric', 'analytic')
    20→            - 'auto': Use analytic if available, otherwise numeric
    21→            - 'numeric': Force numerical differentiation
    22→            - 'analytic': Force analytic (raises error if not available)
    23→            
    24→    Returns:
    25→        Array of derivative values. Shape is (len(t),) for single basis,
    26→        or (len(t), nbasis) for multi-basis HRFs.
    27→        
    28→    Raises:
    29→        ValueError: If method='analytic' but HRF has no analytic derivative
    30→    """
    31→    t = np.asarray(t, dtype=np.float64)
    32→    
    33→    # Check if HRF has analytic derivative method
    34→    has_analytic = hasattr(hrf, '_derivative')
    35→    
    36→    if method == "analytic" and not has_analytic:
    37→        raise ValueError(f"HRF '{hrf.name}' does not have an analytic derivative")
    38→    
    39→    if method == "auto":
    40→        method = "analytic" if has_analytic else "numeric"
    41→    
    42→    if method == "analytic":
    43→        return hrf._derivative(t)
    44→    else:
    45→        return _numeric_derivative(hrf, t)
    46→
    47→
    48→def _numeric_derivative(hrf: HRF, t: ArrayLike) -> NDArray[np.float64]:
    49→    """Compute derivative using numerical differentiation.
    50→    
    51→    Uses scipy.misc.derivative for accurate numerical derivatives.
    52→    """
    53→    t = np.asarray(t, dtype=np.float64)
    54→    n_basis = hrf.nbasis
    55→    result = np.zeros((len(t), n_basis))
    56→    
    57→    # For each basis function
    58→    for j in range(n_basis):
    59→        # Define function for j-th basis
    60→        if n_basis > 1:
    61→            def f(time):
    62→                val = hrf(np.array([time]))
    63→                return val[0, j]
    64→        else:
    65→            def f(time):
    66→                val = hrf(np.array([time]))
    67→                return val[0] if val.ndim == 1 else val[0, 0]
    68→        
    69→        # Compute derivative at each time point
    70→        for i, ti in enumerate(t):
    71→            result[i, j] = derivative(f, ti, dx=1e-6)
    72→    
    73→    # Return as 1D if single basis
    74→    if n_basis == 1:
    75→        return result[:, 0]
    76→    return result
    77→
    78→
    79→# Analytic derivative functions for specific HRF types
    80→
    81→def spmg1_derivative(t: ArrayLike, P1: float = 5, P2: float = 15, A1: float = 0.0833) -> NDArray[np.float64]:
    82→    """Analytic derivative of SPM canonical HRF (SPMG1).
    83→    
    84→    Args:
    85→        t: Time points
    86→        P1: Shape parameter for positive gamma
    87→        P2: Shape parameter for negative gamma  
    88→        A1: Amplitude scaling factor
    89→        
    90→    Returns:
    91→        Derivative values at time points t
    92→    """
    93→    t = np.asarray(t, dtype=np.float64)
    94→    C = 1.274527e-13
    95→    ret = np.zeros_like(t)
    96→    pos = t >= 0
    97→    
    98→    if np.any(pos):
    99→        t_pos = t[pos]
   100→        ret[pos] = np.exp(-t_pos) * (
   101→            A1 * t_pos**(P1 - 1) * (P1 - t_pos) -
   102→            C * t_pos**(P2 - 1) * (P2 - t_pos)
   103→        )
   104→    
   105→    return ret
   106→
   107→
   108→def spmg1_second_derivative(t: ArrayLike, P1: float = 5, P2: float = 15, A1: float = 0.0833) -> NDArray[np.float64]:
   109→    """Analytic second derivative of SPM canonical HRF.
   110→    
   111→    Args:
   112→        t: Time points
   113→        P1: Shape parameter for positive gamma
   114→        P2: Shape parameter for negative gamma
   115→        A1: Amplitude scaling factor
   116→        
   117→    Returns:
   118→        Second derivative values at time points t
   119→    """
   120→    t = np.asarray(t, dtype=np.float64)
   121→    C = 1.274527e-13
   122→    ret = np.zeros_like(t)
   123→    pos = t >= 0
   124→    
   125→    if np.any(pos):
   126→        t_pos = t[pos]
   127→        # Components and their derivatives
   128→        D1 = A1 * t_pos**(P1 - 1) * (P1 - t_pos)
   129→        D2 = C * t_pos**(P2 - 1) * (P2 - t_pos)
   130→        D1_prime = A1 * ((P1 - 1) * t_pos**(P1 - 2) * (P1 - t_pos) - t_pos**(P1 - 1))
   131→        D2_prime = C * ((P2 - 1) * t_pos**(P2 - 2) * (P2 - t_pos) - t_pos**(P2 - 1))
   132→        
   133→        ret[pos] = np.exp(-t_pos) * (D1_prime - D2_prime - (D1 - D2))
   134→    
   135→    return ret

file.content:
"""Derivative methods for HRF objects."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Union
import numpy as np
from numpy.typing import ArrayLike, NDArray
from scipy.misc import derivative

from .core import HRF


def deriv(hrf: HRF, t: ArrayLike, method: str = "auto") -> NDArray[np.float64]:
    """Compute the derivative of an HRF at given time points.
    
    Args:
        hrf: HRF object to differentiate
        t: Time points at which to evaluate the derivative
        method: Derivative method ('auto', 'numeric', 'analytic')
            - 'auto': Use analytic if available, otherwise numeric
            - 'numeric': Force numerical differentiation
            - 'analytic': Force analytic (raises error if not available)
            
    Returns:
        Array of derivative values. Shape is (len(t),) for single basis,
        or (len(t), nbasis) for multi-basis HRFs.
        
    Raises:
        ValueError: If method='analytic' but HRF has no analytic derivative
    """
    t = np.asarray(t, dtype=np.float64)
    
    # Check if HRF has analytic derivative method
    has_analytic = hasattr(hrf, '_derivative')
    
    if method == "analytic" and not has_analytic:
        raise ValueError(f"HRF '{hrf.name}' does not have an analytic derivative")
    
    if method == "auto":
        method = "analytic" if has_analytic else "numeric"
    
    if method == "analytic":
        return hrf._derivative(t)
    else:
        return _numeric_derivative(hrf, t)


def _numeric_derivative(hrf: HRF, t: ArrayLike) -> NDArray[np.float64]:
    """Compute derivative using numerical differentiation.
    
    Uses scipy.misc.derivative for accurate numerical derivatives.
    """
    t = np.asarray(t, dtype=np.float64)
    n_basis = hrf.nbasis
    result = np.zeros((len(t), n_basis))
    
    # For each basis function
    for j in range(n_basis):
        # Define function for j-th basis
        if n_basis > 1:
            def f(time):
                val = hrf(np.array([time]))
                return val[0, j]
        else:
            def f(time):
                val = hrf(np.array([time]))
                return val[0] if val.ndim == 1 else val[0, 0]
        
        # Compute derivative at each time point
        for i, ti in enumerate(t):
            result[i, j] = derivative(f, ti, dx=1e-6)
    
    # Return as 1D if single basis
    if n_basis == 1:
        return result[:, 0]
    return result


# Analytic derivative functions for specific HRF types

def spmg1_derivative(t: ArrayLike, P1: float = 5, P2: float = 15, A1: float = 0.0833) -> NDArray[np.float64]:
    """Analytic derivative of SPM canonical HRF (SPMG1).
    
    Args:
        t: Time points
        P1: Shape parameter for positive gamma
        P2: Shape parameter for negative gamma  
        A1: Amplitude scaling factor
        
    Returns:
        Derivative values at time points t
    """
    t = np.asarray(t, dtype=np.float64)
    C = 1.274527e-13
    ret = np.zeros_like(t)
    pos = t >= 0
    
    if np.any(pos):
        t_pos = t[pos]
        ret[pos] = np.exp(-t_pos) * (
            A1 * t_pos**(P1 - 1) * (P1 - t_pos) -
            C * t_pos**(P2 - 1) * (P2 - t_pos)
        )
    
    return ret


def spmg1_second_derivative(t: ArrayLike, P1: float = 5, P2: float = 15, A1: float = 0.0833) -> NDArray[np.float64]:
    """Analytic second derivative of SPM canonical HRF.
    
    Args:
        t: Time points
        P1: Shape parameter for positive gamma
        P2: Shape parameter for negative gamma
        A1: Amplitude scaling factor
        
    Returns:
        Second derivative values at time points t
    """
    t = np.asarray(t, dtype=np.float64)
    C = 1.274527e-13
    ret = np.zeros_like(t)
    pos = t >= 0
    
    if np.any(pos):
        t_pos = t[pos]
        # Components and their derivatives
        D1 = A1 * t_pos**(P1 - 1) * (P1 - t_pos)
        D2 = C * t_pos**(P2 - 1) * (P2 - t_pos)
        D1_prime = A1 * ((P1 - 1) * t_pos**(P1 - 2) * (P1 - t_pos) - t_pos**(P1 - 1))
        D2_prime = C * ((P2 - 1) * t_pos**(P2 - 2) * (P2 - t_pos) - t_pos**(P2 - 1))
        
        ret[pos] = np.exp(-t_pos) * (D1_prime - D2_prime - (D1 - D2))
    
    return ret

from typing import Union

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Derivative methods for HRF objects."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Union
import numpy as np
from numpy.typing import ArrayLike, NDArray
from scipy.misc import derivative

from .core import HRF


def deriv(hrf: HRF, t: ArrayLike, method: str = "auto") -> NDArray[np.float64]:
    """Compute the derivative of an HRF at given time points.
    
    Args:
        hrf: HRF object to differentiate
        t: Time points at which to evaluate the derivative
        method: Derivative method ('auto', 'numeric', 'analytic')
            - 'auto': Use analytic if available, otherwise numeric
            - 'numeric': Force numerical differentiation
            - 'analytic': Force analytic (raises error if not available)
            
    Returns:
        Array of derivative values. Shape is (len(t),) for single basis,
        or (len(t), nbasis) for multi-basis HRFs.
        
    Raises:
        ValueError: If method='analytic' but HRF has no analytic derivative
    """
    t = np.asarray(t, dtype=np.float64)
    
    # Check if HRF has analytic derivative method
    has_analytic = hasattr(hrf, '_derivative')
    
    if method == "analytic" and not has_analytic:
        raise ValueError(f"HRF '{hrf.name}' does not have an analytic derivative")
    
    if method == "auto":
        method = "analytic" if has_analytic else "numeric"
    
    if method == "analytic":
        return hrf._derivative(t)
    else:
        return _numeric_derivative(hrf, t)


def _numeric_derivative(hrf: HRF, t: ArrayLike) -> NDArray[np.float64]:
    """Compute derivative using numerical differentiation.
    
    Uses scipy.misc.derivative for accurate numerical derivatives.
    """
    t = np.asarray(t, dtype=np.float64)
    n_basis = hrf.nbasis
    result = np.zeros((len(t), n_basis))
    
    # For each basis function
    for j in range(n_basis):
        # Define function for j-th basis
        if n_basis > 1:
            def f(time):
                val = hrf(np.array([time]))
                return val[0, j]
        else:
            def f(time):
                val = hrf(np.array([time]))
                return val[0] if val.ndim == 1 else val[0, 0]
        
        # Compute derivative at each time point
        for i, ti in enumerate(t):
            result[i, j] = derivative(f, ti, dx=1e-6)
    
    # Return as 1D if single basis
    if n_basis == 1:
        return result[:, 0]
    return result


# Analytic derivative functions for specific HRF types

def spmg1_derivative(t: ArrayLike, P1: float = 5, P2: float = 15, A1: float = 0.0833) -> NDArray[np.float64]:
    """Analytic derivative of SPM canonical HRF (SPMG1).
    
    Args:
        t: Time points
        P1: Shape parameter for positive gamma
        P2: Shape parameter for negative gamma  
        A1: Amplitude scaling factor
        
    Returns:
        Derivative values at time points t
    """
    t = np.asarray(t, dtype=np.float64)
    C = 1.274527e-13
    ret = np.zeros_like(t)
    pos = t >= 0
    
    if np.any(pos):
        t_pos = t[pos]
        ret[pos] = np.exp(-t_pos) * (
            A1 * t_pos**(P1 - 1) * (P1 - t_pos) -
            C * t_pos**(P2 - 1) * (P2 - t_pos)
        )
    
    return ret


def spmg1_second_derivative(t: ArrayLike, P1: float = 5, P2: float = 15, A1: float = 0.0833) -> NDArray[np.float64]:
    """Analytic second derivative of SPM canonical HRF.
    
    Args:
        t: Time points
        P1: Shape parameter for positive gamma
        P2: Shape parameter for negative gamma
        A1: Amplitude scaling factor
        
    Returns:
        Second derivative values at time points t
    """
    t = np.asarray(t, dtype=np.float64)
    C = 1.274527e-13
    ret = np.zeros_like(t)
    pos = t >= 0
    
    if np.any(pos):
        t_pos = t[pos]
        # Components and their derivatives
        D1 = A1 * t_pos**(P1 - 1) * (P1 - t_pos)
        D2 = C * t_pos**(P2 - 1) * (P2 - t_pos)
        D1_prime = A1 * ((P1 - 1) * t_pos**(P1 - 2) * (P1 - t_pos) - t_pos**(P1 - 1))
        D2_prime = C * ((P2 - 1) * t_pos**(P2 - 2) * (P2 - t_pos) - t_pos**(P2 - 1))
        
        ret[pos] = np.exp(-t_pos) * (D1_prime - D2_prime - (D1 - D2))
    
    return ret
